framework_name: FFIEC IT Examination Handbook — Outsourcing Technology Services booklet

controls.title: Board and Senior Management Responsibility
controls.description: The board of directors and senior management are responsible for understanding the risks associated with outsourcing arrangements and ensuring effective risk management throughout the relationship lifecycle.

controls.title: Outsourcing Risk Management Function
controls.description: The institution establishes a clearly-defined outsourcing risk management function with documented authority + reporting lines to the board.

controls.title: Information Security Officer Involvement
controls.description: The Information Security Officer is involved in vendor selection + ongoing-monitoring decisions for arrangements involving sensitive customer data or operational impact.

controls.title: Risk Assessment Methodology
controls.description: Outsourcing risk assessments cover: strategic / reputational / operational / transaction / credit / compliance / country risks, prioritized by criticality of the outsourced function.

controls.title: Critical Function Identification
controls.description: The institution identifies its critical business functions and applies enhanced due diligence + monitoring to vendors supporting them.

controls.title: Concentration Risk Analysis
controls.description: The institution assesses concentration risk arising from reliance on a small number of vendors, geographic concentrations, or shared-service-provider relationships.

controls.title: Subcontractor (4th Party) Risk
controls.description: The institution maintains visibility into critical subcontractor relationships used by primary vendors and extends ongoing-monitoring expectations to those parties.

controls.title: Strategic Alignment Review
controls.description: The decision to outsource is documented and reviewed for alignment with the institution's strategic objectives, regulatory obligations, and risk appetite.

controls.title: Cost-Benefit + Risk-Adjusted Analysis
controls.description: The outsourcing business case includes risk-adjusted analysis of in-house vs vendor cost, capability, and quality dimensions.

controls.title: Regulatory Notification (where required)
controls.description: The institution notifies the appropriate federal regulator of material outsourcing arrangements per 12 USC §1867 (BSCA) when applicable.

controls.title: Due Diligence Scope
controls.description: Vendor due diligence covers: financial condition, experience + reputation, business continuity capability, information security posture, regulatory compliance history, insurance coverage, and reference checks.

controls.title: Information Security Posture Review
controls.description: Vendor security posture review covers: encryption + key management, access controls, audit logging, incident response, vulnerability + patch management, third-party audit reports (SOC 2, ISO 27001).

controls.title: Business Continuity + Disaster Recovery Capability
controls.description: Vendor BC/DR capability is evaluated, including documented RTO + RPO targets, tested recovery procedures, and geographic resilience.

controls.title: Pre-Contract Site Visit + Audit
controls.description: For critical relationships, the institution performs an on-site or virtual review of vendor facilities + controls before contract execution.

controls.title: Contract Scope and Performance Standards
controls.description: The contract clearly defines the services in scope, performance standards (SLAs), and remedies for non-performance.

controls.title: Right-to-Audit Clause
controls.description: The contract grants the institution + its regulators the right to audit vendor controls + access vendor records relating to services provided.

controls.title: Confidentiality + Data Protection
controls.description: The contract addresses customer-data protection, confidentiality, breach notification timelines, and data return / destruction at contract end.

controls.title: Subcontracting Restrictions
controls.description: The contract restricts vendor subcontracting (assignment) to require prior institution approval for material transfers; flow-down clauses bind subcontractors to the same protections.

controls.title: Termination + Exit Provisions
controls.description: The contract includes clear termination triggers, notice periods, transition assistance obligations, and data return / portability requirements.

controls.title: Foreign-Based Vendor Considerations
controls.description: Contracts with foreign-based vendors address governing law, jurisdiction, regulatory access, and country-risk factors per FFIEC offshore-outsourcing guidance.

controls.title: Performance Monitoring
controls.description: Ongoing monitoring covers: SLA performance, financial condition, control effectiveness (via SOC 2 / ISO 27001 / right-to-audit), security incidents, regulatory matters.

controls.title: Periodic Risk Reassessment
controls.description: Vendor risk assessments are refreshed on a documented cadence (typically annual for critical vendors; biennial for moderate; triennial for low-impact).

controls.title: Incident + Issue Tracking
controls.description: Vendor-side security incidents, control deficiencies, and SLA misses are tracked + remediated via documented issue-management processes.

controls.title: Change Notification
controls.description: Vendors are required to notify the institution of material changes: ownership, key personnel, subcontracting, service-delivery model, or geographic location.

controls.title: Annual Senior-Management + Board Review
controls.description: Senior management + the board receive at-least-annual reports on the outsourcing portfolio: vendor list, risk ratings, performance, incidents, concentration analysis.

controls.title: Cloud Computing Considerations
controls.description: Cloud arrangements receive enhanced scrutiny for shared-responsibility model clarity, multi-tenancy controls, data residency, and cross-border data flows.

controls.title: Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) Arrangements
controls.description: SaaS arrangements are evaluated for vendor lock-in risk, data portability provisions, and service-availability track records.

controls.title: Resilience + Concentration Across Critical Functions
controls.description: The institution's overall outsourcing posture is reviewed for resilience: avoiding single-point-of-failure vendor dependencies for critical operations.

controls.title: Affiliate + Group-Level Outsourcing
controls.description: Intra-group outsourcing is documented + governed under the same standards as third-party outsourcing, including affiliate transactions per Regulation W.

controls.title: Records Retention + Examination Access
controls.description: Vendor-held records are retained per the institution's retention policy + applicable regulatory requirements; regulators have access via the right-to-audit clause.
